short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $3.42B in FY2026 Q1, +11.5% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $3.30B (-3.5% vs current Q) — set by 28 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $9.40 vs current $9.12.
Margin context: 35.2% net margin — expansion likely.
Next earnings: 2026-07-30 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is constructive — consensus is achievable based on current run-rate.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $13B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $12B → FY2 $14B (+9.8% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: +15.8% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if growth durability + margin expansion both hold.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · trading window)
bullets: Price $1829.47 vs SMA50 $1675.15 (+9.2%) — short-term trend is bullish (price holding above the near-term moving average).
RSI(14): 55.8 — neutral / no edge.
MACD: 30.12 vs signal 42.05 — bearish crossover.
30-day return: +1.0% — directional bias entering the next 4 weeks.
Bollinger band position: 58% of band width (band width 13.7% — normal).
Near-term S/R: resistance ~$1884.35, support ~$1774.59. 91% of 52w range (low $756.88 → high $1935.00).
short_term.takeaway: Short-term trader bias: long (trend + momentum aligned bullish).

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · positioning window)
bullets: Price $1829.47 vs SMA200 $1318.54 (+38.7%) — long-term trend is in **uptrend intact**.
Moving average regime: Golden cross active (SMA50 > SMA200) — institutional trend filter bullish.

90-day return: +21.4% — positive intermediate-term momentum.
long_term.takeaway: Long-term positioning: accumulate on weakness — structural trend supportive.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 53.7% — elevated (premium-selling favored).
Put/Call OI: 1.77 — put-heavy hedging (institutions defensive).
30-day implied move: ±15.1% ($1552.58 – $2106.36) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: +0.33pp — balanced skew.
Front-month OI cluster: $1620.0 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Bull call spread 30 DTE: long $1829 / short $1921. Mid IV (54%) + uptrend → defined-risk directional with reasonable cost basis.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 55.9% across 8 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 8 dates spanning 93–611 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±29.1% ($1297.92 – $2361.02).
393-day implied move: ±57.1% ($784.91 – $2874.03) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $920 (-49.7% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Flat term structure (+2.2pp) — no near-term event premium; long-dated vol fairly priced.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $1647 put / short $2012 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
